one_sentence: An LM change is a code change: gate every prompt/model/retriever change with a test suite of a held-out, curated eval set plus a calibrated metric plus a threshold above measured noise, wired into CI so regressions block the merge.

operation_candidates.name: GenerateEvalCandidates
operation_candidates.trigger: Need a held-out eval set; no labelled fixture exists yet.
action: Generate QA candidates from the corpus (LlamaIndex DatasetGenerator.generate_dataset_from_nodes(num=50), promptfoo synthesis, or task-specific synthesis).
Prefer a different model family than the task model to reduce self-preference leakage.
Tag the pool with generator model id + date; keep it regeneratable.
operation_candidates.output: A raw, unvalidated candidate pool ready for curation.

operation_candidates.name: CurateGoldenSet
operation_candidates.trigger: A generated candidate set exists; it has not been human-reviewed.
action: Human reviews every item: fix wrong labels, drop ambiguous/duplicate/trivially-easy items.
Inject known production failures and adversarial/edge cases the generator missed.
Freeze as a versioned fixture (golden_vN.jsonl); changing it is a versioned event.
operation_candidates.output: A trusted, frozen golden eval set that the gate runs against.

operation_candidates.name: SplitTrainDevTest
operation_candidates.trigger: Eval set built; about to use it for both tuning and gating.
action: Partition into train / dev / test.
Seal test from all prompt-authoring (no demos, instructions, or tuning drawn from it).
The gate reads only test. Note DSPy's reversed 20/80 train/val is an optimizer concern, not the gate's.
operation_candidates.output: Three disjoint splits; a sealed test set for the gate.

operation_candidates.name: SetRegressionThreshold
operation_candidates.trigger: Have a test score; need a pass/fail policy.
action: Commit current test score as baseline.json.
Define absolute floor X (never ship below) and/or relative no-regression delta (PR must not make it worse).
Set delta above measured run-to-run noise; optionally add per-slice floors.
operation_candidates.output: A versioned threshold policy the CI job enforces.

operation_candidates.name: WireCIGate
operation_candidates.trigger: Eval set + metric + threshold exist; not yet enforced automatically.
action: Add a CI job triggered by prompt/model/retriever/graph changes.
Run eval at PR config -> compute metric -> compare to baseline.json -> exit non-zero on breach.
Post the before/after table as a PR comment.
operation_candidates.output: Merges that regress quality are blocked; every change carries a number.
evidence: external: llm regression testing CI, promptfoo

operation_candidates.name: BumpBaselineOnIntendedWin
operation_candidates.trigger: A PR intentionally raises quality; gate would otherwise pin the old baseline forever.
action: In the same reviewed PR, update baseline.json to the new test score.
Never let CI auto-bump silently (would let quality ratchet down unnoticed).
operation_candidates.output: Baseline ratchets upward deliberately; future regressions caught against the new bar.
evidence: dspy: keep versioned per-model artifacts and A/B (line 191)

operation_candidates.name: StabilizeFlakyEval
operation_candidates.trigger: The gate flaps — same config, different verdicts across reruns.
action: Pin temperature=0 and seeds where supported; disable response caching in CI.
Average N runs and gate on the mean; report variance.
If variance > delta: fix the metric (harden judge via metric-design) or widen delta; never disable the gate.
operation_candidates.output: A stable gate whose red builds are trustworthy.

operation_candidates.name: SliceTheEvalSet
operation_candidates.trigger: Aggregate score flat but a subpopulation (lexical queries, a tenant, a topic) silently regressed.
action: Tag eval items by slice.
Compute and gate per-slice; a win on the majority must not mask a regression on a minority slice.
operation_candidates.output: Slice-level regression detection; aggregate no longer hides harm.

dilemmas.name: Generated eval set vs hand-curated golden set
dilemmas.tension: Generation is fast and cheap but inherits the generator model's blind spots, skews easy, and misses real long-tail failures; curation is slow but trusted. Gating on raw generation gives a green build while users churn.
dilemmas.resolution: Generate for coverage (candidate pool only); curate a golden subset (fix labels, drop easy/ambiguous, inject known failures); cross-family generation; size to budget (50 curated > 200 raw); version both pool and golden set.

dilemmas.name: Threshold too strict blocks good changes
dilemmas.tension: A Delta=0 no-regression gate flags within-noise drops as failures, blocking net-positive changes (e.g. cheaper model, equal quality). The team then bypasses the gate, and a bypassed gate protects nothing.
dilemmas.resolution: Measure baseline run-to-run noise (sigma) first; set Delta ~ 2*sigma, not 0; average N runs to shrink the band; track cost on its own axis (cost-tiered-models); if the metric is too noisy to set a sane Delta, harden the metric in metric-design rather than widening Delta to infinity.

anti_patterns.name: Eval set leaks into the prompt (demos/instructions from test)
anti_patterns.fix: Seal test; demos from train only (dspy AP-9)

anti_patterns.name: Gate on a raw generated set
Gate on an uncalibrated metric
anti_patterns.fix: Calibrate via metric-design OP-M05 before gating

anti_patterns.name: Run the gate on the val/dev set used for tuning
anti_patterns.fix: Gate on held-out test only (dspy line 101)

anti_patterns.name: Aggregate-only gate hides a regressed minority slice
Silent baseline auto-bump (quality can ratchet down)
anti_patterns.fix: Bump baseline only in a reviewed PR (OP-06)

anti_patterns.name: Disabling the gate when it flakes

boundaries.action: No production surface to regress (dspy line 259)
Gate only after the I/O contract stabilizes (dspy line 260)

boundaries.rule: No metric possible and none willing to be built
boundaries.action: Route to metric-design first; this skill cannot gate without a metric
Use lm-evaluation-harness; for domain held-out sets descend to domain-eval-set

cross_framework.llamaindex: DatasetGenerator.generate_dataset_from_nodes for the set; Faithfulness/Relevancy/RetrieverEvaluator(mrr,hit_rate) metric; gate-every-change loop; CI wiring is DIY around Evaluate
cross_framework.dspy: explicit train/dev/test (reversed 20/80 for prompt optimizers, held-out test for gate); def metric(ex,pred,trace=None)->bool|float; held-out beats baseline by >= delta; cache=False; CI wiring DIY around dspy.Evaluate
cross_framework.promptfoo: first-class CI: promptfoo eval exits non-zero on failed asserts; assert types equals/contains/llm-rubric/javascript; closest off-the-shelf OP-05
cross_framework.langsmith: managed datasets, evaluator functions / LLM-as-judge, regression alerts and CI integration
